details: Andhra Pradesh Brahmin Welfare Corporation (ABC) Limited, a Government of Andhra Pradesh undertaking is introducing the “Chanakya Scheme for Swayam Upadhi” (CS-SU) to encourage Brahmin entrepreneurs in Andhra Pradesh. Under this scheme, one-time financial assistance upto ₹2,00,000/- (not exceeding 50% of the project cost) for projects costing between ₹3,00,000/- and ₹10,00,000/- will be considered.

To encourage Brahmins to set up Micro Enterprises in Andhra Pradesh in various sectors like Catering, Education, Electronics, Healthcare (Clinic, Diagnostics, Pharmacy, Physiotherapy), Engineering, IT & ITES (Coaching, Development, DTP, Repairs), Photography /Videography, Pooja Materials, Retail, Ritual Tools and equipment, Solar, Telecom, Textiles, etc.
benefits: Not exceeding 50% of the project cost.
eligibility: The applicant should belong to the Brahmin community.
The applicant should reside in any of the 13 districts of Andhra Pradesh.
The applicant should have set up the enterprise in Andhra Pradesh.
The applicant's age should be 50 years or below.
The applicant should have a zeal to become a successful entrepreneur.

online: Applicants have to visit the official website(https://www.andhrabrahmin.ap.gov.in/schemes/schemes.aspx#) and click on "Scheme"
Click on "Registration" After that application form will open
Personal Details: Name, Residence address, Aadhaar no, Ration card no, Bank detail etc
Upload: Photo, Signature and Certificate Details
Documents: Birth Certificate, Residential Certificate, Aadhar Card, Caste Certificate, Bank Passbook etc
After submission receive the reference number.
Click on the “Services” link on the menu and select "Know your status".
Enter the Reference or Mobile or Aadhaar Card Number to get the application status.
Select the "Financial Year" and Fetch the details.
Enter the Aadhaar Card Number and select the "Scheme"

documents_required: Valid Income Certificate (issued by competent authority not earlier than 6 months.
Certificate of Highest Qualification of the Applicant.
Detailed Project Report (DPR) on the Proposed Project.
Write-up covering how his passion, interests, and capabilities match the business proposal.
Bank statement of previous six months.

A reference from a registered Brahmin organization or a Brahmin of eminence (Retired IAS/IPS/IFS Officer, Retired High Court Judge, Retired Professor/VC of any University, Chartered Accountant, Reputed Doctor /Industrialist, Bank employee retired in senior position, etc.), citing his passion, interests, and capabilities.

faqs.question: What is the Chanakya Scheme for Swayam Upadhi (CS-SU)?
faqs.answer: It is a scheme to encourage Brahmin entrepreneurs in Andhra Pradesh by providing financial assistance.

faqs.question: Who implements the CS-SU scheme?
faqs.answer: The Andhra Pradesh Brahmin Welfare Corporation (ABC) Limited.

faqs.question: What is the maximum financial assistance provided under the CS-SU scheme?
faqs.answer: Up to ₹2,00,000/-, not exceeding 50% of the project cost.

faqs.question: What is the project cost range eligible for the CS-SU scheme?
Who can apply for the CS-SU scheme?
faqs.answer: Members of the Brahmin community residing in Andhra Pradesh.

faqs.question: What is the age limit for applicants of the CS-SU scheme?
faqs.answer: Applicants should be below 50 years of age.

faqs.question: Can existing businesses apply for the CS-SU scheme?
faqs.answer: Yes, if they want to develop or diversify their micro enterprise.

faqs.question: What happens after submitting the application for the CS-SU scheme?
faqs.answer: Initial scrutiny and evaluation are done by the State Level Committee (SLC).

faqs.question: Is there an interview process for the CS-SU scheme?
faqs.answer: Yes, applicants will be called for an interview for physical verification of documents.

faqs.question: How is the approved amount disbursed under the CS-SU scheme?
faqs.answer: The amount is paid directly to the vendors.

faqs.question: What is the contact number for assistance with the CS-SU scheme application?
